name: OneDrive Sync

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v1.2.1 — Config File Sync & UX Improvements

### ✨ New Features

- **`.obsidian/` config file sync** — Plugin manifests, app settings, appearance, hotkeys, and community plugin lists now sync bidirectionally with OneDrive when enabled in settings. Changes are detected instantly via Obsidian's file system events.

- **Progress bar notifications** — Sync operations with 5+ files now show a real progress bar (using Obsidian's built-in `ProgressBarComponent`) instead of text-only notices.

### 🛠 Improvements

- **Smarter change detection** — Config files are compared by content hash (FNV-1a), not just modification time. This prevents unnecessary uploads when Obsidian touches config files on startup without changing their content.

- **Cleaner notifications** — Removed redundant "X files synced" and "Everything up to date" popup notices. The status bar already provides this information. Only conflict and error notifications remain.

- **Popout window compatibility** — Timer APIs now use `window.*` methods for compatibility with Obsidian's popout windows. Progress bar falls back gracefully when DOM is unavailable.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fixed config files never uploading despite sync settings being enabled (adapter API fallback for non-TFile paths)
- Fixed config files re-syncing on every plugin reload (startup event suppression + content hash comparison)
- Fixed flaky `logManager` test caused by insufficient microtask flushes
- Removed settings page title heading that caused display issues

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.1.1...1.2.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

## v1.2.0 — Config File Sync & UX Improvements

### ✨ New Features

- **`.obsidian/` config file sync** — Plugin manifests, app settings, appearance, hotkeys, and community plugin lists now sync bidirectionally with OneDrive when enabled in settings. Changes are detected instantly via Obsidian's file system events.

- **Progress bar notifications** — Sync operations with 5+ files now show a real progress bar (using Obsidian's built-in `ProgressBarComponent`) instead of text-only notices.

### 🛠 Improvements

- **Smarter change detection** — Config files are compared by content hash (FNV-1a), not just modification time. This prevents unnecessary uploads when Obsidian touches config files on startup without changing their content.

- **Cleaner notifications** — Removed redundant "X files synced" and "Everything up to date" popup notices. The status bar already provides this information. Only conflict and error notifications remain.

- **Settings page header** — Now shows "OneDrive Sync" as a proper heading with version number and a clickable GitHub link.

- **Popout window compatibility** — Timer APIs now use `window.*` methods for compatibility with Obsidian's popout windows. Progress bar falls back gracefully when DOM is unavailable.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fixed config files never uploading despite sync settings being enabled (adapter API fallback for non-TFile paths)
- Fixed config files re-syncing on every plugin reload (startup event suppression + content hash comparison)
- Fixed flaky `logManager` test caused by insufficient microtask flushes

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.1.1...1.2.0

releases.description:
## What's Changed
* Enhancing README with integration notes and sync benefits by @Copilot in https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/pull/33
* Fix localized app-folder delta paths causing recursive sync trees by @Copilot in https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/pull/36


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.1.0...1.1.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## ⚠️ Breaking Change

This release includes a **new Azure app registration**. Existing users will need to **disconnect and re-link to OneDrive** in the plugin settings after updating.

Your files in OneDrive are not affected — the plugin will re-sync on first connection.

## What's Changed

### App Folder Mode Fixes
- **New app registration** with a clean, consistent folder name (`ObsidianOneDrive`)
- **Dynamic app folder discovery** — the plugin now detects the actual app folder path at runtime instead of relying on a hardcoded constant
- **Fixed first-sync errors** — `.obsidian` subfolder 404 errors are now handled gracefully when the app folder is brand new
- **Fixed upload path construction** — uploads now go directly to the app folder root instead of trying to create `/Apps` from the drive root (which fails with App Folder permissions)

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed `isItemNotFoundError` to catch Graph SDK errors (`GraphError` has `statusCode`/`code` properties but is not an `OneDriveError` instance)
- Improved shared folder support for vaults nested inside shared folders (from 1.0.5)

### UI
- Settings no longer show a hardcoded app folder path — descriptions are now generic since the actual folder name is determined by Azure

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.5

### Bug Fixes

- **Fix shared folder sync when vault is nested inside shared root** — When using a shared OneDrive folder where the vault was in a subfolder (e.g. `SharedFolder/Vaults/MyVault`), sync would fail with a 404 error. Graph API paths were incorrectly constructed relative to the shared root instead of the vault folder. Both delta queries and file operations are now correctly scoped.

### Improvements

- **Refactored sync engine** — Broke up the large `performSync()` method into focused, well-documented helper methods for better readability and maintainability.
- **Centralized timer API** — Extracted shared `timerApi` utility to eliminate `globalThis` references flagged by the Obsidian review bot.
- **Removed all eslint-disable directives** — Cleaner code that passes the Obsidian community plugin review without directive comment errors.
- **Added artifact attestations** — Release assets (`main.js`, `styles.css`) now include cryptographic build provenance via GitHub artifact attestations.
- **Updated README** — Installation instructions now reference the Obsidian Community Plugin Store.
- **Added BRAT pre-release workflow** — Beta testers can now get pre-release builds via BRAT using `manifest-beta.json`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# OneDrive Sync for Obsidian — Initial Release

Sync your Obsidian vault with OneDrive Personal/Consumer. Works on mobile and desktop.

## Features

- **Two-way sync** — local changes upload to OneDrive, remote changes download automatically
- **Delta sync** — uses the OneDrive delta API for fast, incremental syncs (no full scans)
- **Mobile support** — designed for Obsidian Mobile on iOS and Android
- **Conflict resolution** — visual diff viewer for side-by-side conflict resolution
- **Folder-scoped sync** — choose any OneDrive folder as your sync root
- **Selective sync** — `.syncignore` file support for excluding paths
- **Large delete safety** — configurable threshold warns before bulk deletes
- **Plugin & settings sync** — optionally sync community plugin manifests and app settings across devices
- **Device code authentication** — secure OAuth2 device code flow (no passwords stored)
- **Automatic periodic sync** — configurable sync interval with debounced event-driven sync

## Getting Started

1. Install the plugin from the Obsidian Community Plugin directory
2. Open Settings → OneDrive Sync
3. Sign in with your Microsoft account using the device code flow
4. Select a OneDrive folder to sync with
5. Tap "Sync Now" or enable automatic sync

## Requirements

- Obsidian 1.12.0 or later
- A Microsoft account with OneDrive Personal/Consumer storage
